* Woodland MARPAT Digital Camo M65 Field Jacket

##### Product Description

Woodland MARPAT Digital Camo M65 Field Jacket

The M65 field jacket has had a long service record spanning well over 50 years. Originally produced in Olive Drab, these jackets first entered service in the late 1950s, replacing the M51. Some may argue the roots of this iconic design first originated in 1943, with the early production of the M43 field jacket, and they are right! Rock one of America's most historical field jackets!

The jacket was originally designed to provide the wearer with an ideal garment for any climate in a 2 layer system. These jackets come with a cold weather quilted liner, can be removed, and both liner or jacket can be worn as a stand alone jacket. Cool 2 in 1 combo!
* Water-repellent poly/cotton twill outer shell provides protection from wind, rain, and snow.
* Concealable drawstring hood can be easily stored inside the collar with brass zipper closer.
* Four basic outer pockets with snap closer for easy access.
* Heavy duty brass zipper and 6 snap closer storm flap to keep the elements out, adjustable drawstring waist cord for a great conforming fit.
* Hook and loop cuffs

Please remember, when buying military surplus, these items were intended to be used heavily under high intensity conditions. Age and amount of use is a HUGE factor in condition. Unless stated otherwise do not expect a perfect pristine item, it will have its flaws. See conditions below.

* **New Condition** : new with or without tags, various dates and manufacturers.
* **Good Condition** : shows signs of use, light fading/ possible small stains and or small factory repairs. Tags may be legible, may have name tapes and patches sewn on by previous owner. Various dates and manufacturers.
* **Fair Condition** : shows signs of use, fading/ small stains or holes/ factory repairs. Tags may be legible, may have name tapes and patches sewn on by previous owner. Various dates and manufacturers.
* **Poor Condition** : shows signs of heavy use, fading/ stains/ holes/ rips/ major factory repairs.

[Home](https://history-making.com) / [Studio Hire](https://history-making.com/categories/studio-hire/) / United States Marine Corps- MARPAT Woodland Camouflage Uniform- Basic

[](https://history-making.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/USMC-marpat-woodland.jpg)

# United States Marine Corps- MARPAT Woodland Camouflage Uniform- Basic

**MARPAT** (short for **Marine pattern** )is a multi-scale camouflage pattern in use with the United States Marine Corps, designed in 2001 and introduced from late 2002 to early 2005 with the Marine Corps Combat Utility Uniform (MCCUU), which replaced the Camouflage Utility Uniform. Its design and concept are based on the Canadian CADPAT pattern. The pattern is formed of small rectangular pixels of color.
In theory, it is a far more effective camouflage than standard uniform patterns because it mimics the dappled textures and rough boundaries found in natural settings. It is also known as the “digital pattern” or “digi-cammies” because of its micropattern (pixels) rather than the old macropattern (big blobs).

The United States government has patented MARPAT, including specifics of its manufacture.By regulation, the pattern and items incorporating it, such as the MCCUU and ILBE backpack, are to be supplied by authorised manufacturers only and are not for general commercial sale, although imitations are available such as “ **Digital Woodland Camo** ” or “ **Digital Desert Camo** “.

MARPAT was also chosen because it distinctively identifies its wearers as Marines to their adversaries, while simultaneously helping its wearers remain concealed.

* Types of Camouflage Colors: How to Choose the Camo Color for Your Needs

# Types of Camouflage Colors: How to Choose the Camo Color for Your Needs

September 11, 2024 / By [Yuemai](https://www.yuemaioutdoor.com/author/dp_admin/ "View all posts by Yuemai")

[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.yuemaioutdoor.com%2Ftypes-of-camouflage-colors%2F) [](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https://www.yuemaioutdoor.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Types-of-Camouflage-Colors.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.yuemaioutdoor.com%2Ftypes-of-camouflage-colors%2F&description=Types%20of%20Camouflage%20Colors:%20How%20to%20Choose%20the%20Camo%20Color%20for%20Your%20Needs) [](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.yuemaioutdoor.com%2Ftypes-of-camouflage-colors%2F&title=Types+of+Camouflage+Colors%3A+How+to+Choose+the+Camo+Color+for+Your+Needs&source=Yuemai+Outdoor) [](https://wa.me/?text=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.
yuemaioutdoor.com%2Ftypes-of-camouflage-colors%2F)

**Contents** hide

18 Tiger-Stripe Camo

19 Desert Tiger Stripe Camo

20 Gray Tiger Stripe Camo

21 Chocolate Chip Camo

22 Gray Chocolate Chip Camo

23 Ltalian Vegetato Camo

### What is Camouflage Colors?

The origin of camouflage is mainly driven by war, in order to help soldiers quickly integrate into the surrounding environment.
Due to the different combat environments, such as in the desert generally use tan, snow use white and grey, cities use black and grey and other dark tones, forests use green, brown and so on with different color camouflage can achieve different hiding effects. With the development, camouflage color has not only military use, there are hunters, outdoor explorers, outdoor photographers, and so on, thus the color of camouflage is more and more rich.

Next, is our list of different camouflage colors for you, you can observe how many camouflage colors you know and you can choose the camouflage color that suits you according to your needs:

### US Woodland Camo

Also known as M81 Woodland Camouflage, it is a very classic color, mainly consisting of four colors: brown, green, black, and sand, and is mainly used for American troops. Although it has been gradually replaced by other camouflage colors in the middle and late stages of the U.S. military, it is still used by certain special services.

results.title: UCP | Universal Camouflage Pattern — Better Bushcraft
excerpts:
Home UCP | Universal Camouflage Pattern

# UCP | Universal Camouflage Pattern

**UCP Pattern | Universal Camouflage Pattern Explained**

The **UCP pattern** , short for **Universal Camouflage Pattern** , was the digital camouflage design adopted by the U.S. Army in the early 2000s. Also known as **ACUPAT** (Army Combat Uniform Pattern), it debuted in 2004 with the goal of serving as a single, all-environment camouflage solution—usable across **woodland** , **desert** , and **urban** terrains.

### What Is the UCP Pattern?

The **UCP pattern** features a pixelated blend of **grey** , **tan** , and **sage green** , inspired by CADPAT and MARPAT digital camo. Its digital design aimed to obscure a soldier’s outline more effectively than traditional camouflage.

### Key Features of the UCP Pattern:

* **Digital Pixel Camouflage** : Breaks up silhouettes with a computerized, pixelated effect.
* **Three-Color Blend** : Uses grey, tan, and sage green for mid-range concealment.
* **Universal Intent** : Designed to replace multiple camo patterns with a single, standardized look.

### Performance and Criticism:

Despite its promise, the **UCP pattern** faced criticism for underperforming in field tests, especially in **woodland** and **jungle** environments. Studies revealed it offered limited concealment, and by 2015, the U.S. Army replaced it with the more effective **Operational Camouflage Pattern (OCP)** .

### Historical Background:

* **Influenced By** : Canadian CADPAT and USMC’s MARPAT.
* **Launched With** : The Army Combat Uniform (ACU) in 2005.
* **Phased Out** : Officially retired in favor of **OCP/Scorpion W2** due to field performance issues.

**Why the UCP Pattern Matters**

The UCP pattern remains a significant chapter in modern military uniform history. It’s a key example of how digital camouflage evolved—and how even high-tech designs must undergo real-world testing.
While no longer in active use, UCP gear is still widely available through military surplus and appeals to collectors, airsoft players, and tactical gear enthusiasts.

excerpts: The Type 07 "Special Operations Forces Camouflage" pattern (07式特战迷彩作训服) is essentially a pixelated version of the Type 04 DPM camouflage design adopted by PLA special operations units.

excerpts: Utilizing six different color palettes, ... the "Universal" pattern, "Desert/Arid" pattern, "Woodland" pattern, "Oceanic" pattern, "Special Operations Forces" pattern, and an "Urban" pattern. The Type 07 general issue or "Universal Camouflage" pattern (07 式通用迷彩作训服) was intended as a general purpose working and combat design for conventional personnel of the PLA
